metadata.title: Awards, Honours & Persons in News Current Affairs MCQs

questions.question: Who was honoured with the Lifetime Achievement Award at the International Film Festival of Delhi 2026?
questions.explanation: Nandamuri Balakrishna was honoured with the Lifetime Achievement Award at the International Film Festival of Delhi 2026 on March 26, 2026, held at Bharat Mandapam. The award recognises his five-decade-long contribution to Indian cinema, especially his impact on Telugu film industry. Known for his versatility, he has delivered performances in both commercial and character-driven roles, becoming an iconic figure. A key highlight of the event was the screening of his film Bhagavanth Kesari, directed by Anil Ravipudi.

questions.question: Who has become India’s first openly queer Member of Parliament (MP)?
questions.explanation: Senior advocate Menaka Guruswamy took oath as a Member of Rajya Sabha on 6 April 2026. She was nominated by Trinamool Congress and became India’s first openly queer Member of Parliament (MP). She was a key lawyer in the Navtej Singh Johar v. Union of India case leading to decriminalisation of homosexuality under Section 377 of IPC in 2018. Oath ceremony was conducted by Chairman C. P. Radhakrishnan in presence of Kiren Rijiju. She is a Rhodes Scholar from University of Oxford and alumna of Harvard Law School.

questions.question: Susan Coyle has been appointed as the first-ever female Chief of Army of which country in April 2026?
questions.explanation: Susan Coyle has been appointed as the first-ever female Chief of Army of Australia, marking a historic milestone in April 2026, with tenure starting in July. She currently serves as Chief of Joint Capabilities and has nearly four decades of experience since joining the army in 1987. The announcement by Anthony Albanese reflects a shift towards inclusive leadership in defence. She will lead the Australian Army, becoming the first woman to head any service branch. The move aligns with reforms in the Australian Defence Force to improve gender representation and workplace conditions.

questions.question: Who has been elected as the Deputy Chairman of the Rajya Sabha for the third consecutive term?
questions.explanation: Harivansh Narayan Singh has been elected Deputy Chairman of the Rajya Sabha for the third consecutive term, and was elected unopposed. He is the first nominated member to hold this position, having been nominated by Droupadi Murmu after the retirement of Ranjan Gogoi. The motion for his election was moved by J. P. Nadda, Leader of the House in the Rajya Sabha. His unanimous election reflects consensus and cooperation in Indian parliamentary democracy and confidence across party lines.

questions.question: Which institution released the anthology Divya Bharat: A Window to the Soul of India?
questions.explanation: Vice Chairman of NITI Aayog Suman Bery released the anthology “Divya Bharat: A Window to the Soul of India” in New Delhi to promote tourism across India. The book serves as a comprehensive tourism guide, covering all States and Union Territories with details on destinations, culture, cuisine, textiles, and handicrafts. It includes a monthly travel calendar and state-wise chapters, highlighting both famous and lesser-known tourist attractions. The initiative supports the vision of NITI Aayog to strengthen India’s tourism sector and showcase cultural diversity.

questions.question: Oscar Schmidt, who recently passed away, was associated with which sports?
questions.explanation: Oscar Schmidt, one of basketball’s greatest scorers, has passed away at the age of 68 after battling a brain tumor. He had a remarkable 30-year professional career across Brazil and Europe, retiring in 2003 at the age of 45. Schmidt represented Brazil in five Olympic Games and four World Cups, setting all-time scoring records in both tournaments. He was inducted into the Basketball Hall of Fame in 2013, recognising his extraordinary contribution to the sport. The record was surpassed in 2024 by LeBron James, marking a new milestone in basketball history.

questions.question: Who has been appointed as India’s High Commissioner to Bangladesh?
questions.explanation: Dinesh Trivedi has been appointed as India’s High Commissioner to Bangladesh. He is a former Union Minister who served as Railway Minister and Minister of State for Health and Family Welfare. His appointment marks a departure from the usual practice of appointing Indian Foreign Service (IFS) officers to such diplomatic posts. It reflects India’s strategic emphasis on strengthening ties with Bangladesh through political-level engagement.

questions.question: Who received the 9th Prof. M.S. Swaminathan Award 2026?
questions.explanation: Dr Ch Srinivasa Rao, Director and Vice-Chancellor of the Indian Agricultural Research Institute, received the 9th Prof. M.S. Swaminathan Award. The award is instituted by the Retired ICAR Employees Association in collaboration with Nuziveedu Seeds Limited to recognise contributions to India’s food security. It coincides with the birth centenary of M S Swaminathan. Dr Rao was honoured for his work in climate-resilient agriculture and natural resource management. His initiatives have scaled resource conservation technologies across millions of hectares.

questions.question: Who received the 2026 Goldman Environmental Prize for halting commercial fracking along the Magdalena River in Colombia?
questions.explanation: Yuvelis Natalia Morales Blanco, 24, received the 2026 Goldman Environmental Prize for halting commercial fracking along the Magdalena River in Colombia. She co-founded AguaWil, campaigning “No to fracking, Yes to the river.” Fracking, or hydraulic fracturing, extracts oil/gas by injecting water, sand, and chemicals into shale rock. The Goldman Environmental Prize, known as the “Green Nobel,” honors grassroots environmental champions from six regions. The winner of 2026 are all women—include Iroro Tanshi (Nigeria), Borim Kim (South Korea), Sarah Finch (UK), Theonila Roka Matbob (Papua New Guinea), Alannah Acaq Hurley (USA), and Yuvelis Morales Blanco (Colombia).

questions.question: Who became the first woman Chief Justice of Andhra Pradesh High Court?
questions.explanation: Lisa Gill was sworn in as the first woman Chief Justice of the Andhra Pradesh High Court. The oath of office was administered by S. Abdul Nazeer in a ceremony held in Vijayawada. The ceremony included the formal reading of the President’s warrant. The event was attended by senior judges, officials, and dignitaries. This appointment marks an important milestone for gender equality in India’s judicial system.
